items.summary: Birds of a Feather
items.description: Birds of a Feather sessions (or BoFs) are informal gatherings of like-minded individuals who wish to discuss a certain topic without a pre-planned agenda.

items.summary: General Contribution
items.description: If you have experience with Drupal issues and maybe already picked a team/topic, General Contribution is for you. You can join one of the many groups, including: Drupal 9, documentation, migrate, media, front-end, Promote Drupal and more.

items.summary: Prayer Room
Quiet Room
Registration Opens
Scholarship Breakfast
Accessibility audits: The many shapes and sizes. What’s the right fit for you?
items.description: Are you ready to embark on a journey towards digital accessibility excellence? Join us for an informative and practical session that will empower you to run your own accessibility audits, prioritize issues effectively, and take actionable steps towards accessibility compliance.

items.summary: Ethical Implications of Artificial Intelligence
items.description: As artificial intelligence (AI) continues to advance at an unprecedented rate, there is an urgent need to consider its ethical implications. AI has the potential to revolutionize many industries, but it also presents new challenges and risks.

items.summary: Key to Collaboration: How to Build Psychological Safety with Individuals and Teams
items.description: Join us to dive deep into the value of psychological safety and how it supercharges collaboration. We'll break down why it's a game-changer for intra-team relationships, mitigating risk, and facilitating innovation.

items.summary: Mastering Consistency: Expanding content across multiple sites and touch points
items.description: Most higher-ed organizations embrace multi-channel engagement, involving diverse touch points across various media and advertising streams. However, these channels often operate independently, lacking connection in design, content voice and measurement priorities.

items.summary: Save time upgrading from Drupal 7 to Drupal 10 using Retrofit
items.description: With an estimated 400,000 sites facing the mandatory transition to Drupal 10 by 2025, there is an urgent need for efficient, time-saving solutions. Retrofit answers this call. Retrofit provides a backward compatibility layer to run your Drupal 7 code within a Drupal 10 code base.

items.summary: Transforming Drupal into a MarTech LeadGen Machine
items.description: There’s a hard truth that everyone must face when building a website: just because you build it, doesn’t mean the people will come. This also doesn’t mean your next creation won’t be a digital Field of Dreams.

items.summary: Expo Hall Break
Lightning Talk: Trivia with a twist: Stretching mind and body
items.description: Take a step away from the busy sessions and convention halls for some quick trivia while finding your balance and relaxation through provided yoga poses. Engage your mind and body while we guide you through a set of trivia questions coupled with optional entry-level yoga poses!

items.summary: Welcome Remarks and Aaron Winborn Award (Community Working Group Presenting this Award)
Driesnote
items.description: During his keynote - a.k.a. Driesnote - Dries will give one of his engaging keynotes about the state of the Drupal project. This year's address includes a special announcement, counting down to a new chapter in the Drupal project.

items.summary: GROUP PHOTO
Lunch & Expo Hall
Black in Drupal Lunch
Expo Hall Break
Lightning Talk: 5 new free things you get from CKEditor 5 Plugin Pack
items.description: CKEditor 5 Plugin Pack Module is full of free things to help Drupal developers build great applications. • Newly released! Templates are back as a free feature exclusively for Drupal. • Newly released free plugins in the Drupal CK5 Plugin Pack: o Text transformations

items.summary: Certified Partner Roundtable
items.description: Drupal Association partners are invited to a roundtable discussion to learn more about the Drupal Associations strategic plan for the next several years, and how the DA and partners can work together to support the nonprofit mission of the association and build a better open web.

items.summary: Drupal and FOSS CMS Collaboration in a Competitive Market
items.description: In 2023, leadership from the top four FOSS CMS projects (Drupal, Joomla, TYPO3, and WordPress) collaborated for the first time on an open letter, webinar, and other efforts regarding the EU’s Cyber Resilience Act.

items.summary: Drupal Security
items.description: The security team will present topics from their work promoting a more secure Drupal project. The team will also talk about how they recruit new members, refinements to their procedures, and what you can do to support Drupal security.

items.summary: From Many to One: Migrating 70+ Disparate Local Government Websites onto a Cohesive Drupal Platform
items.description: The session will delve into the challenges inherent in the migration process: dealing with large amounts of content, various hosting environments, and multiple data formats.

items.summary: Higher Ed Rebrand or Revamp: Pushing the Envelope Within Your Design System
items.description: While redesigning a website from scratch offers a lot of advantages and flexibility, it isn’t always an option. We often have to work within the constraints of an existing website and an existing brand.

items.summary: Modernizing Oregon Metro's Digital Landscape: A Drupal Journey of Integration, Inclusivity, & Impact
items.description: Join Forum One and Oregon Metro as we unveil the Drupal-driven transformation of Oregon Metro's digital presence.

items.summary: So I logged in, now what? The Dashboard initiative welcomes you
items.description: When you log in into a Drupal Site, by default you land on a mostly empty page which shows you how old your account is in the site. That’s not the best UX out there.

items.summary: Successful Migrations: A Strategic Framework for Planning and Estimating Drupal Migrations
items.description: How much will my migration cost? How long will it take? Planning & estimating a migration from Drupal 7 to D10, or transitioning from other CMSs, present challenges and opportunities that stem from the technical intricacies of each organization's unique needs.

items.summary: The 30-Minute Content Strategist: From Concept to Plan
items.description: Embark on a transformative journey from content chaos to strategic clarity in just 30 minutes. This session is designed to empower attendees with the tools and insights necessary to assess, articulate, and apply a robust content strategy.

items.summary: Expo Hall Break
AI + Atomic Content: Managing Personalized, Omni-channel Content at Scale
items.description: Delivering personalized and relevant customer experiences is key to building strong relationships and driving business growth.

items.summary: Collaborative Editing in Drupal Core
items.description: The team that developed content moderation in Drupal 7 is back with collaborative editing for Drupal 10. Using open-source tools, the Edit Together module provides secure, real-time collaboration inside the Drupal editorial interface.

items.summary: Designing AI Prompts for Content Creation: Balancing User Needs with UX
items.description: 90% of VC-backed companies are planning to bring generative AI into their apps, with 67% aiming to do so this year. Yet according to Jakob Nielsen, only 20% of the population can effectively use it. How can we bridge this divide?

items.summary: Discoveries for Scale: Preparing for Your Brand & Platform Makeover
items.description: Rebrands, acquisitions, and other business decisions have significant impacts on how brands need to adjust their platforms to meet the evolving demands of their organization and their customers.

items.summary: Drupal.org Update
items.description: Join the Drupal.org engineering team for updates on our work to support the community.

items.summary: Drupal's next leap: configuration validation — it's here!
items.description: Hidden in plain sight but unknown to most: Drupal 8.4 added the foundations for validating configuration! Then it took until Drupal 9.4 for actual adoption: the CKEditor 5 module. Since Drupal 10.1, those foundations have been steadily growing! 🚀

items.summary: KineticGPT: Creating an AI assistant for the Kinetic Drupal theme
items.description: What if you could have your own personal AI assistant to quickly answer theme questions, or break down theme structure into easy to understand concepts, or ingest wireframes/designs to kickstart your theme scaffolding?

items.summary: Navigation changes in Drupal’s Admin UI
items.description: One of the big strategic changes we’ll see on Drupal’s Admin UI in the next months is the new toolbar, that will bring several usability improvements beyond the main administration menu and involving several layout changes.

items.summary: Expo Hall Break
Beyond Inclusion: Doing the Right Thing, Drupal-Style
items.description: A sense of belonging makes the difference between who stays and who leaves. Inside Drupal we have a variety of viewpoints and personalities: we have ample opportunities to build strength, empathy, & resilience together.

items.summary: Composable: Discovering Drupal’s next-gen way to structure content
items.description: Introducing Composable, a new module in the Drupal ecosystem that brings the power of creating modular components that can be reused multiple times, in multiple ways, even in different channels.

items.summary: Debugging Yourself: How to Move Forward When the Blocker is You
items.description: In the world of tech, we often find ourselves tangled in a web of bugs, blockers, and 404 errors—but what if the biggest blocker is sitting right in your chair? Yep, we're talking about you.

items.summary: DrupalCon's Next Top Content Model
items.description: Content Models are an important content strategy tool to help clarify requirements and build mutual understanding of all content and relationships within a given system or product.

items.summary: Intermediate Drupal Front End Development (Render Arrays, Debugging Caching, Modern Theming & more!)
items.description: Empower yourself by learning the less obvious, but directly applicable, aspects of Theming with modern Drupal. We'll cover topics including render arrays, caching, debugging your theme, and advanced Twig usage.

items.summary: Navigating Tomorrow: The Future of Websites in the Age of AI and Content Proliferation
items.description: The landscape of websites is undergoing a seismic shift, driven by an insatiable demand for new content and the impending influence of artificial intelligence.

items.summary: Project Browser: On the road to Core
items.description: The goals of the Project Browser Strategic Initiative are to allow users to search for projects (modules) compatible with their website's version of Drupal from within the website itself and to install selected modules with the click of a button.

items.summary: Welcome Party & Expo Opening
Birds of a Feather
items.description: Birds of a Feather sessions (or BoFs) are informal gatherings of like-minded individuals who wish to discuss a certain topic without a pre-planned agenda.

items.summary: General Contribution
items.description: If you have experience with Drupal issues and maybe already picked a team/topic, General Contribution is for you. You can join one of the many groups, including: Drupal 9, documentation, migrate, media, front-end, Promote Drupal and more.

items.summary: Prayer Room
Quiet Room
Recruitment Fair
items.description: Calling all Drupal jobs seekers and employers! Join us in the Expo Hall on Tuesday morning to tour some amazing Drupal organizations who have open roles.

items.summary: Registration Opens
Keynote: Open Source AI Now: Why Open Must Win the AI War
items.description: A battle is underway right now for the future of technology. Artificial intelligence is clearly poised to disrupt every industry. It will change the way we work and live and it will accelerate progress while introducing new perils. Massive change is coming.

items.summary: Expo Hall Break
Lightning Talk: Drupal and Mautic brought together on the Dropsolid DXP
items.description: Dropsolid

items.summary: Case Study: Lenovo's Journey with Drupal and Creating 100+ Language-Specific Sites
items.description: Provide an overview of Lenovo's digital presence and the need for a scalable and multilingual solution. Discuss the complexities involved in creating and maintaining numerous language variations of a website.

items.summary: Community Working Group Roundtable
items.description: The Drupal Community Working Group are volunteers whose goal is to foster a friendly and welcoming environment for all who participate in the Drupal Project.

items.summary: Drupal 11 deep dive: what's new, how to prepare
items.description: Come to this session to learn more about what's new in Drupal 11, what's changed and how to prepare. As of this submission, Drupal 11 is targeted for August 2024 with a fallback of December 2024, so by DrupalCon Portland 2024, we should have a really good idea about it.

items.summary: Drupal Association Public Board Meeting
items.description: Join us for a public board meeting of the Drupal Association. Board members will talk about future programs, conduct regular business to support the Drupal Association's non-profit operations, and answer community questions. By Tim Doyle, Owen Lansbury, Tiffany Farris, and Dries Buytaert.

items.summary: Elevate Your Approval Processes: Mastering Complex Workflows
items.description: We know workflows in Drupal. We've built workflows for many use cases. These include helping non-profits simplify travel approvals, complex academic publishing processes, vetting fundraising proposals, and a system to match newcomers to Canada with appropriate agencies.

items.summary: Getting started using Personalization
items.description: Understanding the real world implications of using Personalization on your website can be tricky - after all there are nearly limitless ways to use it. The reality is that the more you "personalize" your website, the more maintenance it requires to keep your content updated.

items.summary: Sustainable by Design: The revolutionary shift to sustainable web practices
items.description: Sure you know mindlessly scrolling social media is bad for your brain, but it is also terrible for the planet. Those of us who make the internet have a new focus to consider — sustainable web design.

items.summary: Lunch & Expo Hall
Women in Drupal Luncheon | The Lonely Only: Women in tech and navigating a male-dominated landscape
items.description: If you’re a woman in tech, you might know what it feels like to be “the lonely only”. Most of us have been — or are currently — the only woman in our department, or even at our company.

items.summary: Lightning Talk: Is the Redesign Dead?
items.description: ⭐ Lightning Talk: Is the Redesign Dead?

items.summary: Lightning Talk: Domain-specific LLMs the next best step towards Personalization in Beauty Industry
items.description: Domain-specific LLMs are the next best step towards Personalization in Beauty Industry

items.summary: Expo Hall Break
Lightning Talk: Mario Kart Showdown With Third and Grove
items.description: Get ready for a no-holds-barred Mario Kart showdown. Join TAG's reigning Mario Kart champion, Associate Director of Engineering Brandon Stumbo, for an epic battle on the virtual racetrack. Race against Brandon and test your skills for a chance to win some sweet prizes!

items.summary: Automate, Integrate, Innovate: AI-powered GitLab CI for Drupal module development
items.description: In this session, we will embark on a journey into the future of code review, where the integration of ChatGPT and GitLab CI revolutionizes the Drupal module development process.

items.summary: Bringing Personalization Within Reach: Insights From Industry Leaders
items.description: In today's hyper-connected digital landscape, understanding customer behavior and preferences across various touchpoints is paramount for businesses aiming to stay competitive.

items.summary: Drupal Distributions & Recipes Initiative Update
items.description: "[The Recipes Initiative] is a great example of making it easy for people to be part of Drupal and the open web." - Dries Buytaert, Driesnote, DrupalCon Prague

items.summary: Elevating Front-End Excellence: A Fortune 500 Company's Journey with Figma and Storybook
items.description: A team of UX designers and developers, faced with years of design and technical debt reforged their Drupal theme and design documentation around Figma, Storybook and design tokens.

items.summary: How to use automated tools to upgrade your site from Drupal 7 to Drupal 10.x
items.description: 43% of the entire Drupal community is on Drupal 7. Drupal 7 end of life is coming in 2025. Acquia recently open sourced our migration automation tool, Acquia Migrate: Accelerate (AM:A) to help the community get to D10 and prepare for D11.

items.summary: Innovation and Contributions Challenges
items.description: Drupal has not just survived but thrived for more than two decades. One of the key components of this success is with no doubt our community, and all the amazing work that individuals and companies put together to achieve a common goal, a common success.

items.summary: Promoting accessible content throughout the publishing lifecycle
items.description: Accessibility isn’t strictly the responsibility of developers and designers. Arguably the most important, and often forgotten about, area that affects a website’s accessibility is the content itself.

items.summary: Unleashing the power of ECA: No-code coding for ambitious site builders
items.description: The Events, Conditions, and Actions (ECA) module revolutionizes what is possible to do in Drupal without editing code.

items.summary: First-time Contributor Workshop
items.description: Do you want to contribute but are not sure how? Check out the First Time Contributor Pathway for a list of recommended sessions at DrupalCon Portland to help you figure out how to use your particular skills to contribute to the Drupal project!

items.summary: Expo Hall Break
Lightning Talk: PetOps - Keeping Your Pets Safe with Technology
items.description: At amazee.io, we love WebOps, but we're also into PetOps - keeping our precious pets safe! Our furry little coworkers mean the world to us, especially as remote workers.

items.summary: A Migration Is a Terrible Thing to Waste – A roadmap for your next big content migration
items.description: Content migrations are immense efforts that require careful strategic planning, implementation, and testing to ensure your data gets from A to B successfully and intact. Still, they’re also moments of great opportunity.

items.summary: DAM Straight! Why AI is a Clarion Call for Centralized Digital Asset & Content Management
items.description: Wandering into the AI age without a strategy to centralize control over the data, assets and structured content in your content and marketing ecosystem is akin to setting sail into an ocean at night without a rudder, a map, or a lantern.

items.summary: Edit Together: Collaborative Editing for Everyone
items.description: In this case study, we'll showcase Edit Together, the new collaborative editor for Drupal 10.

items.summary: From proposal to partnership; redefining the RFP process.
items.description: Preparing an RFP can be a complex, sometimes painful process that can take months and in some cases, years, of meticulous planning. And after all that careful preparation, it is all too common that the resulting bids include a bewildering range of sometimes-conflicting solutions and prices.

items.summary: Protecting your site with Automatic Updates
items.description: Is your site one of the many Drupal sites running outdated and possibly insecure versions of Drupal core? Come to this session to learn how the Automatic Updates module can help keep your site up-to-date with the latest security updates and make using Composer with a Drupal site easier.

items.summary: Revolutionizing Conversations: AI Chat Integration on YaleSites
items.description: Our condensed five-week journey is an epic tale of innovation and collaboration, where Drupal becomes the canvas for a groundbreaking AI experience.

items.summary: Secure, Performant, Scalable and Green: The big wins of a static Drupal website
items.description: Drupal is the swiss-army-knife of content management systems. It provides the flexibility to build pretty much any site you want. This is why so many of us choose Drupal for our backend technology. **Jamstack vs Static**

items.summary: Using Layout Builder: Practical Advice from the Field
items.description: Layout Builder has been included in Drupal core as a stable module since 2019, yet many site builders and developers still struggle to set it up effectively.

items.summary: Expo Hall Break
Lightning Talk: Decoupled Drupal Do's and Don'ts
items.description: With the rise of GraphQL and the many different frontend technologies available, it seems like now is the time to decouple that Drupal site! Promises of blazing-fast speeds, personalization, and easier development would make any site owner ready to jump in and sign up.

items.summary: Access Control Strategies for Enterprise Drupal Websites
items.description: When working with large organizations—whether in private enterprise, governmental organizations, or nonprofits—editorial responsibility is often spread across multiple sub-organizations. Depending on the type of organization, those subdivisions can look very different:

items.summary: AI readiness: How Drupal can become a leader in the Open Interface Web
items.description: What is the best way to prepare for a world mediated by Artificial Intelligence? What are the threats and opportunities for the Open Web? How can we position Drupal as a leader in AI readiness and the Open Interface Web?

items.summary: How McGill University manages, tests and deploys 1,000 Drupal websites with Ansible and Gitlab CI
items.description: McGill University Web Services manages more than 1,000 Drupal websites hosted on 20 Linux servers that attract 9 million page views per month. This includes the university's main homepage as well as several other highly visible websites. Still, they deploy fearlessly everyday!

items.summary: Migrations for Mortals: Content Migrations for the non-technical folks
items.description: Developers may know the ins and outs of migrations, but what do project managers, designers, or business owners do when faced with working on a large migration project? What makes a migration project different and what should you know when collaborating with developers on such a project?

items.summary: Revolutionizing Drupal with Automated Performance Testing: Deep Dive into Gander, the New Framework
items.description: Drupal's already best-in-class performance is about to get even better thanks to a groundbreaking collaboration between Google and Tag1 Consulting.

items.summary: The Price of Silence: The Hidden Costs of Withholding Feedback in Teams!
items.description: Description:

Prayer Room
Quiet Room

Drupal Project Initiatives Keynote
items.description: Drupal’s initiatives form powerful teams that make great things happen. Some self-organize to get new improvements to the base system, while others coordinate event organization, spread diversity best practices, collaborate on promotional materials, and so on.

items.summary: Expo Hall Break
Lightning Talk: How we used ECA and why you should consider it too
items.description: We had a [relatively] complex set of business rules & requirements to implement and no single core or contributed module could do it all, with just configuration so we used ECA and it worked out.

items.summary: Drupal Branding Panel
items.description: Over the last 12 months, the Drupal Association Board’s Marketing Working Group and Promote Drupal have been working together to update Drupal’s branding.

items.summary: First-time contributor workshop
items.description: Do you want to contribute but are not sure how? Check out the First Time Contributor Pathway for a list of recommended sessions at DrupalCon Portland to help you figure out how to use your particular skills to contribute to the Drupal project!

items.summary: Mentored Contribution
items.description: If you are already familiar with the tools we use such as the issue queue or a local development environment, if needed, but could still use help choosing issues and navigating the process, “Mentored Contribution” is for you.

items.summary: Lunch & Expo Hall
Marketing Contribution
items.description: How can marketers contribute?

Birds of a Feather
items.description: Birds of a Feather sessions (or BoFs) are informal gatherings of like-minded individuals who wish to discuss a certain topic without a pre-planned agenda.

Quiet Room
Registration Opens
Community Summit
items.description: 9:00 - 9:15 am | Introduction, Welcome, and overview. By KathC 9:15 -10:00 am | Panel discussion about Drupal events, led by the Event Organizer Working Group. By Avi Schwab (@froboy), John Picozzi (@johnpicozzi), Nneka Hector (@nneka), & Mike Richardson (@richo)

items.summary: Government Summit
items.description: Thank you for joining us for the Government Summit at DrupalCon Portland 2024! Here you will find the detailed schedule for the day. Please note schedule details are subject to change, and additional content will be added as it becomes available.

items.summary: Healthcare Summit
items.description: 8:30 AM | Check-In 9:00 AM | Welcome & Introduction 9:15 AM | Session 1: Building scalable healthcare content products with Drupal. By Andy Waldrop

items.summary: Higher Ed Summit
items.description: Higher Education Summit Schedule 9:00 - 9:10 | Opening Remarks 9:15 - 9:40 | Sponsor Talk, presented by Axelerant. By Sujatha Varadharajan and Donald Mcleod.

items.description: AGENDA 9 - 9:15 am | Welcome and overview. By Julia Kranzthor 9:15 - 10:30 am | Fireside Chat: Why Should Nonprofits Use Drupal? The Case for Owning Your Own Data and Using Drupal to Manage It. By Tim Lehnen, Johanna Bates, and Jess Snyder 10:30 - 10:45 am | Break

items.summary: TRAINING | Debug Academy
items.description: Debug Academy Introduction to Building Sites with Drupal

items.summary: TRAINING | DrupalEasy
items.description: DrupalEasy Drupal Module Development

items.summary: TRAINING | Evolving Web
items.description: Evolving Web Drupal Theming with SDC and TailwindCSS

items.summary: HIGHER ED SUMMIT | Case Study: University of East London, a tale of a successful digital partnership
items.description: In this session we will discuss how we continue to enable the Vision 2028 for University of East London in building an impactful, diverse, future-focused, careers-led university through our long standing partnership.

items.summary: HIGHER ED SUMMIT | Empower Content Editors: Effective End-User Training and Documentation Strategy
items.description: Transitioning to a new platform can be very exciting and overwhelming for editors, especially those who may not have a technical background or expertise.

items.summary: HIGHER ED SUMMIT | How Acquia and Drupal Power Robust, Modern, and Secure Digital Experiences
items.description: In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, higher education institutions face the dual challenge of managing complex website infrastructure while ensuring security and governance.

items.summary: HEALTHCARE SUMMIT | Building healthcare content SaaS products with Drupal
items.description: Drupal has proven to be a powerful CMS for building ambitious website experiences. But what about using it for building SaaS applications specific to healthcare?

items.summary: GOVERNMENT SUMMIT | Creating next gen government websites that your customers crave
items.description: Learn how one of the largest utility companies in the world completely changed how people use a utility website powered by Drupal.

items.summary: HIGHER ED SUMMIT | How Drupal Rescued Georgia Tech’s International Students During and Post-Pandemic
items.description: I'm thrilled to share my experience with you about how Drupal played a crucial role in transforming Georgia Tech's traditional, in-person advising platform for international students into a virtual platform during the COVID-19 pandemic.

items.summary: Lunch
HIGHER ED SUMMIT | How to Design Under the Constraints and Bureaucracy of Higher Education
items.description: Any design project will have its constraints - and for good reason! But what happens when you’re designing with and for higher education institutions, each with its own unique blend of people, opinions and let’s face it - bureaucracy?

items.summary: HIGHER ED SUMMIT | Harvard College: Don't Call it a Redesign
items.description: Harvard College updated their print materials for student recruitment and wanted a way to bring that new look to the website efficiently and economically.

items.summary: Trivia Night
items.description: Trivia Night is happening, steps from the convention center at the Spirit of 77 500 NE Martin Luther King Jr Blvd, Portland, OR 97232
